title: Calories Burned Swimming (By Stroke, Intensity, and Body Weight)
introduction: Swimming can burn significant calories while being joint-friendly. Your burn depends heavily on stroke choice and intensity. Here are practical estimates to guide your training and fat-loss plan.

sections.heading: 🏊 Quick Answer: Swimming Calorie Burn Per 30 Minutes
sections.content:
| Stroke / Intensity | 150 lb (68 kg) | 180 lb (82 kg) | 210 lb (95 kg) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Leisure freestyle | 180-240 kcal | 220-290 kcal | 250-340 kcal |
| Moderate laps | 240-330 kcal | 290-390 kcal | 340-450 kcal |
| Vigorous freestyle | 330-430 kcal | 390-520 kcal | 450-600 kcal |
| Butterfly / hard sets | 380-500 kcal | 460-610 kcal | 530-700 kcal |

Use the [Calorie Burn Calculator](/tools/calorie-burn-calculator) to estimate your exact burn.

sections.heading: Why Swimming Burn Varies So Much
sections.content:
| Factor | Burn Impact |
| --- | --- |
| Stroke technique | Better technique can improve efficiency |
| Rest intervals | More rest lowers average burn |
| Water temperature | Cooler water may increase energy use |
| Session structure | Intervals often increase total burn |

Two swimmers doing 30 minutes can have very different calorie totals.

sections.heading: How to Increase Calories Burned Swimming
sections.content:
Try this progression:

• Add short high-effort sets (e.g., 8 x 50m)
• Reduce rest gradually
• Alternate strokes to recruit more muscle
• Track total active swim time

Structured sessions are usually more effective than casual unplanned laps.

sections.heading: Using Swimming for Weight Loss
sections.content:
Swimming supports fat loss when paired with intake control.

| Key Action | Why It Matters |
| --- | --- |
| Estimate burn realistically | Avoid over-crediting workouts |
| Keep protein intake high | Supports muscle retention |
| Track intake daily | Prevents post-swim overeating |

Track meals and workouts in Eati so your pool sessions translate into real progress.

midArticleCta: Use the Calorie Burn Calculator for your stroke and intensity, then track intake in Eati to keep your weekly deficit consistent.
conclusion: Swimming can be an excellent calorie-burning workout, especially with structured intervals and consistent effort. Estimate your burn realistically, then combine that with meal tracking in Eati for better fat-loss outcomes.
metaTitle: Calories Burned Swimming: Stroke-by-Stroke Burn Chart
metaDescription: Find calories burned swimming by stroke, intensity, and body weight. Practical swimming burn tables for fat-loss and training planning.

faqs.question: Does swimming burn more calories than running?
faqs.answer: It depends on intensity, duration, and body weight. Vigorous swimming can rival running, but moderate running often burns more per minute for many people.

faqs.question: Why am I so hungry after swimming?
faqs.answer: High energy output and water temperature effects can increase appetite. Planning post-workout meals helps keep your calorie deficit on track.
